chapter_title.en: Chapter on Advance Payment in Food, Animals, and Other Goods

text: : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَسْلَفَ رَجُلاً دَرَاهِمَ بِحِنْطَةٍ حَتَّى إِذَا حَضَرَ اَلْأَجَلُ لَمْ يَكُنْ عِنْدَهُ طَعَامٌ وَ وَجَدَ عِنْدَهُ دَوَابّاً [دَوَابَّ] وَ رَقِيقاً وَ مَتَاعاً أَ يَحِلُّ لَهُ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ مِنْ عُرُوضِهِ تِلْكَ بِطَعَامِهِ قَالَ "نَعَمْ يُسَمِّي كَذَا وَ كَذَا بِكَذَا وَ كَذَا صَاعاً".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3939 - Safwan bin Yahya narrated from Al-Ays bin Al-Qasim, who said:
I asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a man who had advanced dirhams to another man for wheat. When the due date arrived, the debtor did not have any food but had animals, slaves, and goods.
I asked: "Is it permissible for him to take these items in place of the wheat owed to him?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, he may specify such and such items for such and such a measure."
